scenario_title: Prerequisite exists at RRset versus name level on empty non-terminal

group_1.behavior: Treats the empty non-terminal as a name in use, so the prerequisites pass and the TXT RRset is added.
group_1.post_state: QUERY mid.campus.edu. TXT returns the newly added TXT RRset.

group_2.behavior: Treats the empty non-terminal as not satisfying the name-level prerequisite, or maps the mismatch to a prerequisite failure.
group_2.post_state: QUERY mid.campus.edu. TXT returns no TXT RRset.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.1 defines name-level and RRset-level prerequisites, but it does not precisely specify whether an empty non-terminal counts as "in use" for name_in_use / name_absent, nor how that should reconcile with rrset_absent on the same name. RFC 2181 §5.2 discusses name/data semantics but does not resolve UPDATE prerequisite evaluation for empty non-terminals, so implementations may legitimately diverge on whether the UPDATE succeeds.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: The zone contains only a descendant RR under mid.campus.edu., making mid.campus.edu. an empty non-terminal. The single UPDATE simultaneously asserts a name-level existence condition and an RRset-level absence condition at that empty non-terminal, then attempts to create a TXT RRset there. This directly probes the underspecified mapping between name existence and RRset existence in RFC 2136 and should produce either different UPDATE rcodes or a different post-update TXT query result across servers.
